DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Richard Levine, Esq. 310-503-1589 for Plaintiff.
Tara M. Steeley, Esq. 415-554-4655 for Defendant. (In-person)
Court reporter: Nicole Jacobs, CSR #13655, 415-597-5600, Schedule@behmke.com

ARGUED. Ms. Steely submits to the tentative ruling. Mr. Levine contests Plaintiff has not sufficiently alleged that it failed to exhaust administrative remedies. Ms. Steeley responds.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF POLICE COMMISSIONERS,and WILLIAM SCOTT DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco et al's demurrer to Plaintiff San Francisco Police Officers' Association's First Amended Complaint is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Nicole Jacobs, CSR #13655, 415-597-5600, Schedule@behmke.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Continued To Jun-18-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2025 Continued To Apr-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Richard Levine, Esq. 310-393-1486 Raines Lucia Stern St. Phalle & Silver, PC for Plaintiff. (In-person)
Tara Steely, Esq. 415-554-4655 Deputy City Attorney for Defendant.
Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR# 4609, 877-479-2484, reportersolutions@uslegalsupport.com

Argued. Plaintiff Mr. Levine contests and argues. The Court comments, poses questions and offers leave to brief on issues raised in the tentative ruling - declaratory relief standard, standing and exhaustion; Deputy city attorney presents arguments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF POLICE COMMISSIONERS,and WILLIAM SCOTT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The Court vacates the tentative ruling and continues the hearing to 4/24/25 at 9am. Briefing schedule: Plaintiff's brief is due by 3/27/25, Defense' brief is due by 4/3/25. Hearing is set for 4/24/25 at 9am in Dept. 302, subject to Mr. Levine checking his calendar. If the hearing date does not work, parties are ordered to meet and confer and present 3 stipulated dates to the court via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR# 4609, 877-479-2484, reportersolutions@uslegalsupport.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2025 Continued To Mar-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF POLICE COMMISSIONERS,and WILLIAM SCOTT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Continued to March 13, 2025, on the Court's own motion.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Continued To Feb-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF POLICE COMMISSIONERS,and WILLIAM SCOTT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendants City and County of San Francisco, Board of Police Commissioners and Police Chief William Scott's demurrer is continued to February 27, 2015, at 9:00 am in this department. Plaintiff San Francisco Police Officers' Association's objection to the Bar Association of San Francisco's amicus brief is overruled. Plaintiff's objection to the San Francisco Public Defender's amicus brief is overruled, provided that the San Francisco Public Defender's office provide a courtesy copy of its papers to the department no later than February 7, 2025, at noon. Each party to the case may file a supplemental brief not to exceed 15 pages of text (e.g., not including tables) responding to the amicus briefs. Such briefs must be filed no later than 4:00 pm on February 19, 2025, with a courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing. All involved are reminded that this is a demurrer in which the record is limited to the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed and the legal question is limited to whether challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
